title: fix: respect safe=false for nanobanana, replace prompt prefix with safetySettings
author: pollinations-ai[bot]

gist.headline: Taking the training wheels off nanobanana
gist.blurb: We fixed a bug that was being a little too overprotective with nanobanana image generation. Now, when you ask for safe=false, you actually get it—plus better image quality without those clunky prompt injections.
gist.summary: Fixed an issue where the safe=false parameter was ignored for nanobanana models, and replaced context-heavy prompt injections with native Vertex AI safety settings.
gist.impact: Users will experience fewer false-positive content blocks on creative prompts and improved overall image quality when using the nanobanana model.
gist.visual_concept: The PR is about removing overprotective barriers from the 'nanobanana' model. Polly the bee flipping a 'SAFE: FALSE' switch, allowing a glowing pixel-art banana to radiate creative energy without restrictions.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot flipping a chunky retro switch labeled 'SAFE: FALSE' on a glowing terminal. Next to the terminal, a glowing pixel-art banana radiates colorful, creative energy, breaking free from a small glass dome. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
